verses.text: 'A king shall reign in righteousness,' and each ruler be 'as an hiding place from the wind'; eyes that see shall not be dim. A just reign shelters the people. This righteous King points to Christ, the true refuge and light.

'A king shall reign in righteousness,' and each ruler be 'as an hiding place from the wind'; eyes that see shall not be dim. A just reign shelters the people. This righteous King points to Christ, the true refuge and light.

'A king shall reign in righteousness,' and each ruler be 'as an hiding place from the wind'; eyes that see shall not be dim. A just reign shelters the people. This righteous King points to Christ, the true refuge and light.

Then the vile shall no longer be called noble; the churl's schemes are evil, but 'the liberal deviseth liberal things.' Character is unmasked. In the kingdom of the King, the generous and the selfish are each seen for what they are.

Careless women at ease are called to tremble, for harvest will fail and trouble come. Complacency is rebuked. Those who rest secure in their comfort are warned of coming loss.

They shall mourn for the ruined fields, and the palaces shall lie forsaken 'until the spirit be poured upon us.' Desolation lasts till revival. The land's barrenness waits for the breath of God.
They shall mourn for the ruined fields, and the palaces shall lie forsaken 'until the spirit be poured upon us.' Desolation lasts till revival. The land's barrenness waits for the breath of God.
They shall mourn for the ruined fields, and the palaces shall lie forsaken 'until the spirit be poured upon us.' Desolation lasts till revival. The land's barrenness waits for the breath of God.

When the Spirit is poured from on high, the wilderness becomes a fruitful field, and 'the work of righteousness shall be peace.' The Spirit transforms. Where God's Spirit comes, righteousness springs up and yields lasting peace.

When the Spirit is poured from on high, the wilderness becomes a fruitful field, and 'the work of righteousness shall be peace.' The Spirit transforms. Where God's Spirit comes, righteousness springs up and yields lasting peace.

God's people shall dwell in peaceable habitations and quiet resting places; blessed are they that sow beside all waters. Peace and plenty follow. The Spirit-blessed life is secure, restful, and fruitful.
